notes: Lost one full buy-in overall; the KK vs AA all-in preflop (-$200) was an unavoidable cooler, but even stripping that out, ~$108 of leak damage accumulated primarily from UTG (-$174 across 19 hands), a 21% showdown win rate, and c-bets failing at 0% on highcard and paired boards. KJs floated to a king-high showdown (-$13) and AK call-call-folded the river (-$34), both symptoms of passive postflop play without a clear plan.
studyFocus: 1. Fix UTG leak — opened ATo (not in UTG range which is 77+/AJs+/KQs/AQo+); one leak hand but reflects a range discipline gap. 2. Stop defending BB with trash — 94o twice and A8o once, all losers; these are on the explicit avoid list. 3. C-bet fold equity at 4.3% on fold-to-cbet means opponents are floating and raising you; need better texture selection or give up c-betting without equity. 4. 3-bet rate at 0.9% is too low — add 3-bets to defend BB and squeeze steals.

notes: Short session driven by two big losing hands: QJh trips running into a likely full house on a QTsQc2s board (-$120, turn raise + river barrel both called off), and K9s second-pair call-down on a J-K-Q-9-4 wet board (-$43). UTG continued to bleed (-$94 over just 9 hands) and 0% 3-bet rate meant no preflop aggression to build pots or protect range; all c-bets on dry boards failed with zero folds from opponents.
studyFocus: 1. QJh UTG is in range (QJs is a UTG open) — the -$120 loss was a postflop spot; study whether calling off trips vs large turn+river bets is correct given board texture Q-T-Q-2-J. 2. BB defend leaks: A2o and 83s are both off the corrected GTO chart — fold these vs tight opens. 3. 0% 3-bet rate continuing from session 1 — this is a pattern that needs addressing; at minimum: 3-bet squeeze obvious BTN/SB steals with KQs, TT+, or A5s/A4s. 4. Fold-to-cbet still 0% — either stop calling flops without equity or float with a plan to raise turn.

notes: Winning session (+$84.15, +85.09 BB/100) at Mahomet $0.50/$1.00, 99 hands, final stack $184.15. Result driven by multiple winning hands including AQo BTN (+$40.49), AQs BB 3-bet pot (+$85.69), and KK8 value (+$32+). KK vs broadway -$32.64 on 9h8dJd-3c-Ac was one of few losses. Structural leaks continue: PFR 12.1% is too low vs GTO target of ~18-22% (hero calling too wide instead of raising); steal rate only 36.4% with 7 missed steal spots including A4o, T9o, K6o from CO/BTN/SB. C-bet succeeded 0 out of 4 attempts — same as sessions 1+2, opponents floating/raising everything; fold-to-cbet stayed at 4.5% (opponents are never folding to hero c-bets). Showdown win rate 22.2% (4/18) — too many weak hands reaching showdown. Positive: 3-bet rate improved to 5.1% (from 0% in session 2), hero 3-bet with KK, KQs, AQs — appropriate value hands.
studyFocus: 1. PFR too low at 12.1% vs GTO target ~18%+ — hero VPIP/PFR gap is 15 pts (27.3/12.1); too many flat-calls instead of raises. Open-raise from CO/BTN instead of limping/overcalling; default steal from SB with any two cards vs weak BB. 2. Missed 7 steal spots: A4o CO, T9o CO, K6o SB, 8-3o SB, T8s SB, 4-5o BTN, 2-4o BTN — should open most of these. 3. BB defend range leak: A4o and 85s called vs UTG (neither is in the vs-UTG call range); tighten to the validated chart. 4. C-bet: 0% success rate persists across all 3 sessions — either size up on dry boards (75%+ pot) or check back turn/river without equity. Stop 3-barrel bluffing into stations. 5. Fold-to-cbet 4.5% is exploitatively low — float less on flops without clear equity or bluff-catching plan. 6. KK spot: review calling off on 9-8-J-3-A board after 3-bet preflop; consider pot control on wet board when facing aggression.
